channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Blue city police sergeants say they're paid less than subordinates as billions go to migrants
item.description: NYPD sergeants are seeking a new contract with New York City as many make less than the rank-and-file officers they supervise, union president says.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Private jets collide at Scottsdale Airport in Arizona, killing 1 person, authorities say
item.description: SCOTTSDALE, Ariz. (AP) — One person was killed and others were injured when a private jet owned by Mötley Crüe singer Vince Neil collided with another jet Monday afternoon at the Scottsdale Airport in Arizona, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: SEJAL GOVINDARAO and HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: America's kids are still behind in reading and math. These schools are defying the trend
item.description: COMPTON, Calif. (AP) — Math is the subject sixth grader Harmoni Knight finds hardest, but that's changing. In-class tutors and “data chats” at her middle school in Compton, California, have made a dramatic difference, the 11-year-old said.
item.dc:creator: ANNIE MA and JOCELYN GECKER, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Roses are red, violets are blue, 940 million flowers are traveling (through Miami) to you
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — If any husbands or boyfriends mess up Valentine's Day this week, it's not because of a shortage of flowers. In the run up to Feb. 14, agricultural specialists at Miami International Airport have processed about 940 million stems of cut flowers, according to U.S.
item.dc:creator: DAVID FISCHER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Corruption survey gives many nations worst scores in over a decade. South Sudan slides to the bottom
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — Many countries had their worst showing in more than a decade in an index released Tuesday that serves as a barometer of public sector corruption worldwide, from leading powers such as the United States and France to authoritarian nations such as Russia and Venezuela.
item.dc:creator: GEIR MOULSON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Migrants stranded in Mexico try to restart life after Trump eliminates legal pathway to US
item.description: TIJUANA, Mexico (AP) — Margelis Rodriguez and her two children took selfies on their flight to Tijuana, showing off the T-shirts she had custom-made to mark what she expected to be her family's life-changing moment.
item.dc:creator: JULIE WATSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump's education chief comes from the boardroom, not the classroom. Backers say that's her strength
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Linda McMahon once described herself as an outsider stepping into the world of education, a business leader with an “inquiring mind” who would challenge the status quo and demand more for students.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Hawaii court rules against insurance companies in Maui wildfire, allowing $4B settlement to proceed
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — Hawaii’s Supreme Court ruled Monday that insurance companies can’t bring their own legal actions against those blamed for Maui’s catastrophic 2023 wildfire, allowing a $4 billion settlement that was on that the verge of collapse to proceed.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER SINCO KELLEHER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Private jets collide at Scottsdale Airport in Arizona, killing at least 1 person, authorities say
item.description: At least one person was killed and others were injured when private jets collided Monday afternoon at the Scottsdale Airport in Arizona, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump’s Department of Homeland Security looking to deputize IRS agents for immigration enforcement
item.description: DHS Secretary Kristi Noem sent a letter to U.S. Treasury secretary Scott Bessent, asking that IRS agents be provided to assist with immigration and enforcement efforts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Combat your post-Super Bowl fatigue with these freebies, discounts
item.description: Even though the Super Bowl is over, several restaurants and chains are offering freebies to help football fans recover from Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Super Bowl 'flu'? Record number didn't go to work Monday, poll suggests
item.description: “May be calling out sick tomorrow,” one Eagles supporter wrote on X on Sunday night. “Got that birds flu.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, NewsNation

item.title: Prosecutor says Salman Rushdie was too stunned to react when a man started to stab him
item.description: MAYVILLE, N.Y. (AP) — Salman Rushdie was so stunned when a masked man started to stab him on a stage in western New York that the author didn't even try to fight back, a prosecutor said Monday during opening statements in the suspect's attempted murder trial.
item.dc:creator: CAROLYN THOMPSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Los Angeles family featured in Super Bowl commercial
item.description: LOS ANGELES (KTLA) – Taco Bell marked its return to the Super Bowl with a commercial that featured nearly 400 fans, including a couple from Los Angeles and their newborn baby.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Deputies reunite with family they saved from crash with stolen vehicle
item.description: “It was a mother and two children. At which point I drove in front of the vehicle causing the vehicle to crash into me," said Connor Lacey.
item.dc:creator: Alliyah Sims, NewsNation

item.title: Officer-involved shooting investigated at Tennessee travel center
item.description: Preliminary information obtained by the Tennessee Bureau of Investigation states that deputies responded to the business after a reported stabbing.
item.dc:creator: Quametra Wilborn, NewsNation

item.title: Trump: Palestinians wouldn't have right to return to developed Gaza
item.description: "No, they wouldn't, because they're going to have much better housing. Much better," Trump told Fox News's Bret Baier in an interview.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Former Buckeyes football coach Jim Tressel nominated as Ohio's lieutenant governor
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — Ohio Gov. Mike DeWine on Monday nominated former Ohio State Buckeyes football coach Jim Tressel to be the state's next lieutenant governor, praising him as a seasoned educator and leader with the skills to take the state's helm if need be.
item.dc:creator: JULIE CARR SMYTH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Space telescope spots rare 'Einstein ring' of light around galaxy in our cosmic neighborhood
item.description: Europe’s Euclid space telescope has detected a rare halo of bright light around a nearby galaxy, astronomers reported Monday.
item.dc:creator: MARCIA DUNN AP Aerospace Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Video: Rare ‘Black Demon’ fish spotted alive for first time
item.description: The scary-looking fish is usually found in water depths of 1,500 meters, where little to no sunlight reaches.
item.dc:creator: Celeste Houmard, NewsNation

item.title: Member of Kendrick Lamar's Super Bowl halftime show won't face charges for flag protest
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — The cast member who displayed the Palestinian flag with the word “GAZA” in protest during Kendrick Lamar's Super Bowl halftime show will not face charges, New Orleans police said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Search turns up ghost guns, psychedelic mushrooms in Orange County
item.description: A suspect identified as 50-year-old Blair Brockman of Mission Viejo was arrested in connection with the investigation
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, NewsNation

item.title: 6 hidden messages in Kendrick Lamar's Super Bowl performance
item.description: Kendrick Lamar’s Super Bowl halftime performance is growing in viewership hours after the big game, gaining more than 11 million views since it aired Sunday night.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, WGN 9

item.title: Blue state Democrats spearhead bills to crack down on campus antisemitism
item.description: More than a year after the Oct. 7 Hamas terror attack on Israel sparked U.S. campus protests, New York lawmakers propose a crackdown on hate.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Super Bowl LIX highlights: Memorable plays, moving tributes and a historic presidential visit
item.description: The Philadelphia Eagles defeated the Kansas City Chiefs 40-22 in the first Super Bowl to be held in New Orleans in a dozen years. It was played amid heavy security — and with President Donald Trump in attendance — just 41 days after a man plowed a truck into New Year's revelers on Bourbon Street.
item.dc:creator: Brett Martel, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Senate Democrats launch portal for whistleblowers in Trump administration
item.description: Senate Democratic Leader Chuck Schumer (D-N.Y.) and Sen. Gary Peters (D-Mich.), the ranking member of the Senate Homeland Security Committee, said they are launching a new portal for whistleblowers to expose wrongdoing and abuses of power in the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump tells Treasury Department to stop making pennies
item.description: President Trump claims he has instructed the U.S. Treasury Department to stop producing pennies, calling the 1-cent coin wasteful.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Black educators warn of what is lost if Education Department is shuttered
item.description: Black educators are sounding the alarm as President Donald Trump ramps up talk of eliminating the Department of Education.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, KRON4

item.title: Hawaii is the rainbow capital of the world. Here's what that means
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — Hawaii's regular sunshine, short rain showers and clean air come together to create some of the planet's best conditions for viewing rainbows. They're such a frequent sight that a University of Hawaii professor calls the state the “rainbow capital of the world.
item.dc:creator: AUDREY McAVOY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Jon Batiste's reimagined Super Bowl anthem highlighted wife's painting and set stage for new music
item.description: Jon Batiste transformed the Super Bowl stage into a symphony of creativity, debuting new music, reimagining “The Star-Spangled Banner" and shining a global spotlight on his wife's creative vision.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN LANDRUM Jr., Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Remains of all 10 people killed in Alaska plane crash have been recovered: authorities
item.description: The remains of all 10 people killed when their small plane crashed into ice on the Bering Sea have been recovered, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: What makes a dog a winner? A Westminster Kennel Club judge explains
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The competitors can come in forms as different as a Chihuahua, a great Dane, a bulldog and a greyhound. Each one is a champion.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Is PSN still down? PlayStation Network addressing ongoing outage
item.description: Thousands of users have reported outages with the PlayStation Network since Friday evening but as of Saturday morning things still didn't seem back normal.
item.dc:creator: Dominique Jack, Fox 5

item.title: A tax credit worth up to $7,830 often goes unclaimed: See who qualifies for the EITC
item.description: The IRS estimates that a fifth of eligible taxpayers fail to take advantage of the tax break each year.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KRON4

item.title: 2 million donut products recalled nationwide over listeria risk: FDA
item.description: More than 2 million cases of baked goods distributed throughout the nation are being recalled this year due to the possible presence of listeria monocytogenes.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Self, Fox 5

item.title: Elon Musk perched behind Trump's desk in new TIME Magazine cover
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk is seen perched behind President Donald Trump's desk in the Oval Office in their latest cover.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, Fox 5

item.title: The US stopped allowing passport gender marker changes. Here are some of the people affected
item.description: The day after President Donald Trump returned to office, Lisa Suhay took her 21-year-old daughter, Mellow, to a passport office in Norfolk, Virginia, where they live. Getting a passport for Mellow, who is transgender, was urgent.
item.dc:creator: GEOFF MULVIHILL and JOHN HANNA, Associated Press, ABC 4
